user.title: Tuya Lock Monitor v2 — Connection Mode
user.description:
Choose how to connect to your smart lock.

**Cloud (recommended):** Uses the Tuya IoT Platform. Requires a free account and a project at iot.tuya.com. Can optionally add a local IP for faster local polling.

**Local only:** No cloud account needed. Enter your device's local key directly. The local key can be found in your router's logs or retrieved once via tinytuya's setup wizard.

cloud.title: Tuya Lock Monitor v2 — Cloud Setup
cloud.description:
Enter your Tuya IoT Platform credentials.

1. Log in at **iot.tuya.com**
2. Go to **Cloud → Development → My Cloud Projects**
3. Open your project → **Overview** tab
4. Copy **Access ID** and **Access Secret**
5. Find your **Device ID** in **Cloud → Development → Devices**

**Local IP** (optional): Add your lock's LAN IP for fast local polling (15s) instead of cloud-only polling (60s). Find it in your router's DHCP table.

data.local_ip: Lock LAN IP address (optional — enables local mode)

local.title: Tuya Lock Monitor v2 — Local Only Setup
local.description:
Choose how to find your lock on the network.

**Scan** broadcasts a discovery packet on your LAN and finds Tuya devices automatically — it will pre-fill the IP address and Device ID for you.

**Manual** lets you enter all details yourself if the scan doesn't find your device.

local_scan.title: Tuya Lock Monitor v2 — Discovered Devices
local_scan.description:
Select your lock from the list below. The IP address and Device ID will be pre-filled on the next screen — you only need to enter your **local key**.

If your lock is not listed, select **Enter manually** at the bottom.

local_manual.title: Tuya Lock Monitor v2 — Local Connection Details
local_manual.description:
Enter your lock's local connection details.

**How to find the local key:**
- Run `python -m tinytuya wizard` on any PC on the same network (requires a one-time Tuya IoT Platform login to retrieve the key)
- Or check your HA debug logs from a previous cloud-mode setup — search for `local_key`

**Device ID:** Shown in the Tuya/Smart Life app under device details, or found by the tinytuya wizard.

**Device IP:** Find it in your router's DHCP client table, or use the Scan option.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the device. Check the IP address and that the device is on the same network.
error.invalid_auth: Invalid Access ID or Secret. Check your Tuya IoT Platform credentials.
error.local_failed: Could not communicate with the device. Check the local key and protocol version.
error.no_devices_found: No Tuya devices were found on your network. Make sure the lock is powered on and connected to Wi-Fi. Click Submit to enter details manually instead.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Check the HA logs for details.

init.title: Tuya Lock Monitor v2 — Settings
init.description:
Update local connection settings.

**User names** are configured via a single shared file, `tuya_lock_users.yaml`, placed in your Home Assistant config directory. Its format is:

```yaml
fingerprint_names:
  1: Pat
  2: Alex
password_names:
  1: Front door code
card_names:
  1: Key fob
```

Every lock set up with this integration reads the same file, so fingerprint slot 2 always resolves to the same person across all locks.

**Users YAML path** (optional): Override the location of that file. Leave blank for the default (`<config>/tuya_lock_users.yaml`).

For cloud mode, enter the lock's LAN IP to enable fast local polling (15s). Leave blank for cloud-only (60s).

data.local_key: Local key (update if device was reset and re-paired)
